title: RouteEntry hop_count not bounded during construction
description:
File: python/src/lichen/loadng/cache.py
Lines: 25-38

The RouteEntry dataclass accepts any int for hop_count but the wire protocol limits it to 0-255 (single byte):
```python
@dataclass
class RouteEntry:
    destination: IPv6Address
    next_hop: IPv6Address
    hop_count: int  # No bounds check
    metric: int     # No bounds check
    seq_num: int    # No bounds check
    valid_until: int
```

Problem: The messages.py validates seq_num at serialization time, but RouteEntry stores these values without validation. This can lead to:
1. Inconsistent data in the cache
2. Logic errors when comparing metrics (negative values)
3. Serialization failures when cache entries are converted to messages

Impact: Invalid values can propagate through the routing system and cause unexpected behavior during path selection or message generation.

Fix: Add validation in __post_init__:
```python
def __post_init__(self) -> None:
    self.destination = to_ipv6(self.destination)
    self.next_hop = to_ipv6(self.next_hop)
    if not 0 <= self.hop_count <= 255:
        raise ValueError(f'hop_count out of range: {self.hop_count}')
    if not 0 <= self.metric <= 0xFFFF:
        raise ValueError(f'metric out of range: {self.metric}')
    if not 0 <= self.seq_num <= 0xFFFF:
        raise ValueError(f'seq_num out of range: {self.seq_num}')
```
close_reason: Fixed: Added bounds validation in RouteEntry.__post_init__ for hop_count (0-255), metric (0-65535), and seq_num (0-65535). Invalid values now raise ValueError at construction time.
